text:
History & Background Namal University traces its roots to 2005, when Imran Khan conceived the vision to build an academic institution on the banks of Namal Lake, Mianwali, with the goal of empowering Pakistan's youth regardless of socio-economic background. Namal College was established in 2008 with support from the local community and generous donors, beginning as an affiliate college of the University of Bradford, UK. The initial 5 acres of land were donated by Ghulam Muhammad Seelu, a local resident of village Namal.

In early 2019, Namal was granted degree-awarding status and became Namal Institute. The institution has since grown into a fully-fledged university and is part of the larger Namal Knowledge City initiative — an ambitious project to create Pakistan's first knowledge economy hub in the heart of the Salt Range.
heading: 1. About Namal University

text:
"To become a center of academic excellence for national uplift and development through educating bright youth with Namal values, and finding innovative solutions to rural challenges through highly trained academics."

Namal Knowledge City Namal Knowledge City is an ambitious project modeled on globally renowned knowledge hubs such as Oxford and Cambridge. It aims to be the first project of its kind in Pakistan, bringing together knowledge workers from around the world. The campus sits on 1,000 acres in Pakistan's Salt Range, overlooking Namal Lake. The development plan includes academic blocks, student hostels, faculty apartments, a central library, a mosque, an enterprise center, and commercial areas. Sports facilities, a Talent Advancement Center, and an expanded academic block are planned for completion by March 2026.

Campus & Location Namal University is located 30 km along Talagang Road in Mianwali, Punjab 42250, Pakistan. The scenic campus sits at the foothill of a mountaintop overlooking Namal Lake — a 5 sq km natural lake surrounded by the Koh-i-Suleiman and Salt Range. The valley is a sanctuary for migrant bird species and the landscape embraces the Green Namal philosophy of environmental stewardship.

heading: 2. Leadership & Governance

text:
Department of Electrical Engineering Established to produce quality engineers meeting market demand, the EE department offers a BS Electrical Engineering programme (136 credit hours). The department features highly qualified faculty, state-of-the-art laboratories, and a vibrant research environment. The curriculum covers engineering, physical sciences, communications, humanities, and social sciences, with elective tracks in AI/ML, Emerging Hardware Technologies, Communication & Networks, and Power Systems. Vision & Mission: Vision: To become a center of excellence in teaching and application-oriented research.

Mission: To produce technically competent, trustworthy graduates and carry out application-oriented research for the socio-economic development of rural Pakistan.
heading: 3. Academic Departments

text: Department of Computer Science The Computer Science department offers a BS Computer Science programme (133 credit hours) focusing on theoretical foundations of computation and practical software development. The curriculum is designed to ground students in computing fundamentals while equipping them with skills to tackle local and global challenges. Research areas include Machine Learning, NLP, AI, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, and Software Engineering.

Department of Business Studies (Namal Business School) The BBA programme at Namal Business School provides an international perspective on business concepts coupled with practical knowledge of Agribusiness, Business Analytics, Marketing, and Finance. The programme emphasizes community development, rural uplift, and producing socially responsible graduates.
heading: CS Faculty:

text: Department of Mathematics The BS Mathematics programme (128 credit hours) is HEC-accredited and covers core mathematics, enabling students to develop analytical and quantitative reasoning skills. Research areas include Computational Fluid Dynamics, Algebraic Topology, Geometry, Mathematical Modeling of Infectious Diseases, and Applied Mathematics.

heading: Mathematics Faculty:

text: Eligibility Criteria Applicants must have at least 70% marks in Intermediate/equivalent examinations. Result-awaiting candidates may apply on the basis of first-year marks (conditional offer). Non-Matriculation/Intermediate students must provide an equivalence certificate from IBCC.
heading: 4. Admissions

text: Merit Criteria Namal University aggregate merit is calculated as: 43% Intermediate/equivalent marks + 43% Entry Test score. Admissions are offered based on first preference; subsequent preferences are considered after exhausting higher ones.
heading: Programme-wise Qualification Requirements:

text: 1. Access the Namal Online Admission Portal and register with your personal email. 2. Activate your account via email verification. 3. Complete your personal profile. 4. Select 1st, 2nd, and 3rd programme preferences. 5. Provide Matric/Intermediate/Entry Test details. 6. Pay the application fee of Rs. 1,000/- (non-refundable) via MCB, JazzCash, EasyPaisa, or Online Banking. 7. Upload a valid Entry Test result and educational documents. text:
Namal University firmly believes that quality education is the right of every talented student, regardless of financial background. Generous merit and need-based financial support is available.

Fee Policies Admission Fee and Security Fee are one-time charges paid at the time of admission. Tuition Fee is collected on a quarterly basis. Those who cannot afford the fees may apply for financial support through the university's scholarship process.

Nisar Aziz AgriTech Center (NAAC) NAAC focuses on innovative agriculture, rural entrepreneurship, and quality research to improve farmers' livelihoods through sustainable agricultural productivity, cost reduction, farm mechanization, and livestock production.

Vision: To be the leader in innovative sciences, rural uplift, and improved livelihoods through sustainable agricultural practices in Pakistan. The NAAC team is led by Dr. Muhammad Ashraf (Head) with members including Dr. Azhar Rasool, Mr. Saleem Ullah (Soil Chemist), and Mr. Saqib Aziz (Community Mobilizer).

text:
Namal Alumni Association (NAA) Namal University aims to establish a strong Alumni Association to strengthen bonds with graduates and engage them in university uplift. The association focuses on student/alumni development, academia-industry linkages, endowment fund, and academic advisory.

Organizational Structure The association comprises an Executive Body, a General Body, a Steering Committee (Namal Alumni Association Steering Committee – NAASC), and Assisting Committees. The Rector serves as Chief Patron.
heading: 7. Alumni Association

text:
Alumni General Elections The Executive Body of the Namal Alumni Association is democratically elected every 3 years via the online Namal Alumni Portal. Positions include: President (1), Vice President (1), Secretary (1), Treasurer (1), Batch Representatives (13).

Strategic Themes (Terms of Reference) Alumni Networking: Establish close liaison with alumni, organize meetings, reunions, and homecomings; promote alumni achievements. Academia–Industry Relations: Build working relations with industry for internships, job placements, trainings, guest talks, and industrial visits.

Student Development/Mentorship: Liaise with departments and placement center to assess training needs and engage resource persons from industry/academia. Endowment Fund: Raise and manage funds to support university development and scholarships.

heading: Notable Alumni Stories

text:
Green Namal Initiative Green Namal is the university's commitment to environmental sustainability. The initiative motivates all members of the Namal family to become ecologically conscious. The university uses renewable energy resources, recycles waste, and actively maintains its scenic natural surroundings including Namal Lake (5 sq km), the historic Namal Dam (built 1913 by the British), and the surrounding valley — a sanctuary for migratory birds including Russian ducks and Siberian cranes.
